compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Decision (per scope): implemented the genuine end-to-end path (not the reframe). CLI block captures the created id via $(rebar create ... | tail -1) and threads the SAME ticket through init->create->ready->claim->close; no jq needed (plain create prints the id as its last stdout line). Verified the block runs end-to-end in a throwaway git repo. MCP block reframed in prose as server config (not implying an operation). This is the runnable block sibling 0435 extracts+executes in CI.

compiled_state.description:
## What

The `README.md` quickstart (lines ~33-53) claims to "Run one ticket end-to-end
across all three surfaces" but does not: the CLI block creates a ticket then acts on
an unrelated hard-coded id `reel-lot-tea`; the Python block creates a **separate**
ticket; the MCP block shows only client `mcpServers` config and performs no
operation. This misleads every audience about how rebar actually works.

## Scope

**Make it genuinely end-to-end** (this is now the required path, not one of two — see
below): the CLI quickstart block must capture the created id from `create`
(e.g. `--output json` piped to a shell variable) and thread that SAME ticket through
`ready` → `claim` → `close`, so the block is a coherent, copy-pasteable, runnable
sequence with NO hard-coded disconnected id. The Python block threads the same intent;
the MCP block must either perform a real operation or the prose must stop implying it
does.

**Why the reframe/per-surface-examples alternative is no longer admissible:** sibling
`0435` (golden-path CI gate) EXTRACTS the README quickstart's CLI fenced block and
executes those exact lines in a throwaway repo. That requires a single coherent runnable
command sequence in the block. A "disconnected per-surface examples" reframe would leave
0435 with no coherent block to extract/run. So this ticket MUST deliver the runnable
end-to-end CLI block. (`0435` `depends_on` this ticket, so it lands after this fix.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The quickstart's heading/claim matches what the code blocks actually do.
- [ ] No hard-coded id (`reel-lot-tea`) that is disconnected from a just-created
      ticket remains in a block that implies continuity.
- [ ] The CLI quickstart block is a runnable single-ticket sequence (captured id
      flows create → ready → claim → close) with no disconnected hard-coded id, and is
      exercised verbatim by the golden-path CI smoke (sibling `0435`, which `depends_on`
      this ticket).

Files: `README.md` (lines ~33-53).
compiled_state.title: P0: Repair README quickstart into a real end-to-end run
